{"society_power": 1.5, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -1.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Post-Tiananmen repression plus fiscal recentralization and growth-based legitimacy", "reasoning": "Fiscal recentralization (1994 tax reform), Deng's Southern Tour, smooth succession, and growth-based legitimacy strengthened an already dominant Despotic Leviathan, while sustained crackdowns (Tiananmen aftermath, China Democracy Party, Falun Gong) demobilized nascent organized society despite modest economic autonomy from marketization."}
